_meta.purpose: Per-circuit lap-level reference data for sim: race lap count/distance, a representative reference laptime, full-throttle percentage (straight-line-ness), and an estimated fuel burn per lap. Fills three placeholders flagged in SIMLOG.md: DEFAULT_BASE_LAP_TIME_SEC=90s in undercutOvercut.ts/strategyCompare.ts, fuelBurnPerLapKg/startFuelKg generic constants in fuel.ts, and the flat activeAeroDragDeltaPct/estimatedLapTimeGainSec in ers.ts (which should become track-straight-proportion-aware).

confidenceLegend.confirmed: Directly stated by an official/primary source, independently verified in this pass.
confidenceLegend.reasonable_estimate: Derived from documented facts (circuit length, publicly reported full-throttle %, general F1 lap-record knowledge) but not independently re-verified per circuit against a primary source in this pass, or computed via an explicitly documented formula rather than measured.
confidenceLegend.placeholder: No usable source or estimate basis yet; a plain default the sim should not treat as calibrated.

_meta.raceLapsAndDistanceMethod: Sourced from the 2024 F1 season's official race distance/lap counts (a Yahoo Sports 2024 season recap article compiling the FIA-published per-race lap counts). Lap counts almost never change year-to-year for an unchanged circuit (they're set once to satisfy the ~305km minimum race-distance rule), so these are used as the 2025 figures too, but that carry-forward itself is NOT independently reconfirmed for 2025 in this pass -- hence 'reasonable_estimate' rather than 'confirmed' even though the underlying 2024 figures are solid. circuitLengthKm is calculated as raceDistanceKm / raceLaps and cross-checked against independently known circuit lengths (e.g. Bahrain 5.41km, Monaco 3.34km, Spa 7.00km) -- all matched within rounding, which validates the source data.
_meta.referenceLapTimeMethod: referenceLapTimeSec is each circuit's official F1 Grand Prix lap record (the fastest race lap ever set at that layout) -- a fixed, low, real number that gives sim/undercutOvercut/strategyCompare a track-differentiated baseline instead of the flat 90s placeholder. This is NOT a 'typical race pace' laptime (real race laps run several seconds slower once tyre deg/fuel load are modeled on top by sim's own degradation.ts/fuel.ts) -- it's a floor/reference value, same role the flat 90s placeholder was playing. Only Bahrain and Monaco were independently re-verified against Wikipedia in this pass (both matched general knowledge exactly); the remaining circuits' exact record times are from general F1 knowledge and are NOT independently re-verified per-circuit here, so all except Bahrain/Monaco are tagged reasonable_estimate. Recommend sim treat the whole field as directionally reliable (correctly rank-orders tracks, e.g. Monaco << Silverstone << Spa) but re-verify exact digits before treating any single value as broadcast-accurate.
_meta.fullThrottlePctMethod: Percentage of the lap spent at full throttle -- a real, commonly-published F1 technical-preview stat (Mercedes/Pirelli circuit guides). Anchored to several confirmed public figures (Monza >80%, Jeddah 79%, Silverstone 70%, Suzuka ~66%, Hungaroring ~53-61%, Singapore 49%, with Monaco and Mexico City reported lower still) and interpolated for the remaining circuits using the same circuit-characteristic reasoning as track-tyre-characteristics.json's abrasivenessRating (straight length/count, chicane density, street vs permanent). All values reasonable_estimate except where a source figure is cited directly in a circuit's basis string.
_meta.fuelPerLapKgMethod: No single published per-circuit fuel-consumption table exists (same gap noted for pit-loss/SC data in tracks.json). Computed via an explicit, documented formula rather than invented per-circuit: fuelPerLapKg = 0.30 * circuitLengthKm + 0.15 + (fullThrottlePct - 60) / 100 * 0.4 -- i.e. a lap-length-scaled base (longer lap burns more fuel) plus a throttle-intensity adjustment (higher full-throttle % burns more fuel per km than a low-speed technical lap). This is an invented-but-transparent methodology, calibrated only to land in the commonly-cited real-F1 range of ~1.0-2.3kg/lap (Monaco lowest, Spa/Las Vegas/Jeddah highest) -- explicitly a reasonable_estimate for every circuit, not measured data. Sim's fuel.ts can multiply by raceLaps to get a track-specific startFuelKg instead of the flat 110kg placeholder.
_meta.overtakingDifficultyMethod: Qualitative low/medium/high/very_high tier + basis string, added per ai teammate's request (2026-07-10) for a sourceable ReferenceFact to replace their hand-written Monaco placeholder text in why-not-alternative explanations. Reasonable_estimate, drawn from well-documented circuit reputation (DRS zone count/placement, straight length, corner width) rather than a single cited statistic -- same confidence tier as the abrasiveness ratings.
_meta.cornersMethod: corners = official/widely-published turn count per circuit (F1.com/Wikipedia circuit guides), added 2026-07-11 to let this file fully supersede src/mocks/lapsAndCorners.ts (which was missing shanghai and madring entirely, and still had barcelona at its pre-2023 16-corner count -- corrected to 14 here after independent verification). Only shanghai, barcelona, and madring were independently re-verified this pass (confirmed); the remaining 22 are reasonable_estimate widely-published figures carried over from general knowledge, consistent with (and cross-checked against) the values visual had already been using via lapsAndCorners.ts.

referenceLapTimeSec.basis: F1 GP lap record, ~1:19.813 (Leclerc, 2024, current post-2022 reprofiled layout).

fullThrottlePct.basis: 2022+ layout removed several chicanes in favour of flowing corners, raising full-throttle time markedly vs the old street-adjacent layout.

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Wide, modern layout with multiple DRS zones post-reprofile; better overtaking than its old chicane-heavy reputation.

corners.basis: Current post-2022 layout turn count, widely published circuit spec.

fullThrottlePct.basis: Long back straight offset by a tight, twisty infield section (Turns 1-3 spiral, Turn 14 hairpin).

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Long back straight into a hairpin is a reliable overtaking spot; rest of lap is tighter and harder to pass on.

fullThrottlePct.basis: Publicly reported ~66% full throttle (high-speed Esses/130R offset by the tight chicane/hairpin).

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Narrow, high-speed, historically one of the hardest calendar circuits to pass on outside the final chicane/main straight.

referenceLapTimeSec.basis: F1 GP lap record, 1:31.447 (Pedro de la Rosa, McLaren MP4-20, 2005 Bahrain GP) -- independently verified via Wikipedia 2026-07-10.

fullThrottlePct.basis: Long start/finish straight and back straight, generous run-off desert layout.

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Multiple wide, heavy-braking zones off the two long straights; designed with overtaking in mind.

corners.basis: Widely published circuit spec (Grand Prix layout).

referenceLapTimeSec.basis: F1 GP lap record, ~1:30.734 (Verstappen-era pace, 2021-2025 range).

fullThrottlePct.basis: Publicly reported ~79% full throttle, ~155mph+ average speed -- one of the highest-speed circuits on the calendar.

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Very high average speed makes overtaking committal and wall-proximity risky despite reasonable straight-line opportunities.

corners.basis: Widely published circuit spec -- most corners of any circuit on the calendar.

fullThrottlePct.basis: Mixed layout: long back straight (Turns 16-17 run) offset by a tight stadium chicane section (Turns 14-17).

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Long straight into Turn 11 hairpin is a reliable DRS pass; stadium section is tight and pass-resistant.

fullThrottlePct.basis: Fast, flowing old-school layout (Tamburello/Villeneuve straights) with few slow corners.

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Narrow, wall-lined circuit with very few genuine overtaking spots (Tamburello/Variante Alta straights are the main chances).

referenceLapTimeSec.basis: F1 GP lap record, 1:12.909 (Lewis Hamilton, Mercedes W12, 2021 Monaco GP, current post-2015 layout) -- independently verified via Wikipedia 2026-07-10.

fullThrottlePct.basis: Lowest full-throttle circuit on the calendar -- reported lower than Hungaroring's ~53%, tightest street layout with no genuine straight.

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Definitionally the calendar's hardest circuit to overtake on -- track width barely exceeds car width in most sections; near-universally cited in F1 broadcast/technical coverage.

corners.basis: Widely published circuit spec (current post-2015-layout count).

fullThrottlePct.basis: Mix of high-speed sweepers (old Turns 3, 9) and a tighter, DRS-straight-added final sector.

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Widely regarded as overtaking-difficult despite DRS zones -- high-speed, aero-sensitive corners punish following closely.

circuits.notes: Replaced on the 2026 calendar by Madring; retained for 2025-relevant F1 25 base-game modeling.

corners.basis: MotoGP-derived final-sector layout used by F1 since the 2023 Spanish GP (removed the old chicane); older sources citing 16 are the pre-2023 layout. Independently verified 2026-07-11.

fullThrottlePct.basis: Stop-start layout of long straights into heavy-braking chicanes.

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Multiple long straights and heavy braking zones (Turn 1, hairpin) make it one of the calendar's better overtaking circuits.

fullThrottlePct.basis: Short lap dominated by three long uphill/downhill straights.

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Short lap with three genuine straight-into-heavy-braking overtaking zones; consistently produces high overtaking counts.

corners.basis: Widely published circuit spec -- fewest corners of any calendar circuit.

fullThrottlePct.basis: Publicly reported ~70% full throttle (Maggotts-Becketts-Chapel high-speed sequence dominates the lap).

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Wide, fast, flowing layout with several straight-into-braking-zone passing spots (Brooklands, Stowe, Hangar Straight into Village).

fullThrottlePct.basis: Longest circuit on the calendar, dominated by the Kemmel Straight and other high-speed sections.

fuelPerLapKg.basis: Formula-derived, see _meta.fuelPerLapKgMethod -- highest fuel-per-lap on the calendar given its length.

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Eau Rouge/Raidillon into the Kemmel Straight is one of F1's most famous overtaking zones; consistently high-overtaking circuit.

fullThrottlePct.basis: Publicly reported 53% of lap time / 61% of lap distance at full throttle; used as a blended ~58% for this field's single-number convention.
fullThrottlePct.source: web search of Mercedes-AMG F1 circuit-guide style coverage

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Tight, twisty, low-average-speed 'Monaco without the walls' -- historically one of the calendar's hardest circuits to pass on.

fullThrottlePct.basis: Narrow dunes circuit with banked corners but limited pure straight length.

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Narrow, banked, limited run-off; consistently rated one of the harder modern-calendar circuits to pass on.

corners.basis: Widely published circuit spec (includes the two banked corners).

referenceLapTimeSec.basis: F1 GP lap record, 1:20.901 (Norris, 2025, breaking Barrichello's long-standing 2004 record of 1:21.046).

fullThrottlePct.basis: Publicly reported >80% full throttle -- the 'Temple of Speed,' highest full-throttle circuit on the calendar.
fullThrottlePct.source: web search of F1 technical-preview coverage

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Lowest-downforce, highest-speed layout on the calendar with three chicane-into-braking-zone passing spots; historically high overtaking counts.

corners.basis: Widely published circuit spec -- lowest-corner-count high-speed layout.

fullThrottlePct.basis: One of the longest straights on the calendar (2.2km) offset by a very tight, low-speed castle-section old-town sector.

overtakingDifficulty.basis: The long straight is a reliable DRS pass; the castle section is single-file and pass-resistant.

referenceLapTimeSec.basis: F1 GP lap record, ~1:41.905 (post-2023 layout-shortening era).

fullThrottlePct.basis: Publicly reported ~49% full throttle -- one of the lowest on the calendar.
fullThrottlePct.source: web search of F1 technical-preview coverage

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Narrow, walled street circuit with very few genuine passing zones; historically one of the calendar's hardest-to-pass venues alongside Monaco.

corners.basis: Widely published circuit spec, current post-2023 (Turn 16-17 removal) layout.

fullThrottlePct.basis: Purpose-built modern layout blending a fast uphill Turn 1 esses section with a long back straight.

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Long back straight (Turn 11/12) into a hairpin is a consistent DRS pass; rest of lap is more technical.

fullThrottlePct.basis: Publicly reported among the calendar's lower full-throttle circuits, grouped with Monaco/Singapore/Hungary -- thin high-altitude air reduces effective drag but the stadium section is tight.
fullThrottlePct.source: web search of F1 technical-preview coverage

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Very long main straight is a strong DRS pass, but the tight stadium section (Turns 12-15) is single-file and pass-resistant.

fullThrottlePct.basis: Short, undulating lap with a decent-length start/finish straight (uphill) balanced by a tighter infield.

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Short lap, uphill straight into Turn 1, and frequent mixed-weather races combine to make this a historically good overtaking/racing circuit.

fullThrottlePct.basis: Dominated by the ~1.9km Strip straight and other long straights; very high full-throttle proportion, similar tier to Monza/Jeddah.

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Very long straights favour DRS passing, offset by a tighter, low-grip street-circuit final sector.

fullThrottlePct.basis: High-speed, sweeping MotoGP-derived layout with few slow corners.

overtakingDifficulty.basis: Flowing, high-speed layout with limited hard-braking zones makes overtaking possible but not easy.

referenceLapTimeSec.basis: F1 GP lap record, ~1:26.103 (Verstappen, 2021, current post-2021-reprofile layout).

fullThrottlePct.basis: 2021 reprofile widened several corners and shortened the old hotel-section chicane, raising full-throttle time and overtaking chances vs the pre-2021 layout.

overtakingDifficulty.basis: The 2021 reprofile was specifically designed to improve overtaking versus the old processional layout; meaningfully easier to pass on since.

corners.basis: Widely published circuit spec, current post-2021-reprofile layout.

raceLaps.basis: No race run yet (first race Sept 2026). Estimated from the ~305km standard minimum race distance / stated 5.4km circuit length; not confirmed.

referenceLapTimeSec.basis: No race or qualifying session run yet; no basis for even an informed estimate.

fullThrottlePct.basis: Rough Miami-hybrid-circuit analogue per tracks.json's existing 'comparable to Miami's hybrid feel' note -- not an independent estimate.

fuelPerLapKg.basis: Formula-derived from the placeholder circuitLengthKm/fullThrottlePct above; inherits their placeholder status.

overtakingDifficulty.basis: No historical races run yet at time of writing (2026-07-10).

circuits.notes: New to the 2026 calendar / F1 25: 2026 Season Pack, replaces Barcelona as Spanish GP host. See tracks.json for full circuit notes.

corners.basis: Already sourced in tracks.json circuits[].notes for this circuit (5.4km, 22 corners), reused here for consistency rather than re-deriving.
